attributes.description: Alerts when a non-advisory destination holds more than 1000 unconsumed messages. A deep queue indicates the consumer cohort cannot keep up with producer rate — the classic consumer-lag signal.
attributes.name: [ActiveMQ OTel] Queue depth high

investigation_guide.blob:
## ActiveMQ queue depth high

### What fired
The gauge `activemq.message.current` on a user destination (DLQ and advisory destinations excluded) exceeded 1000 over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Growing queue depth is the canonical signal that consumers are falling behind producers. Effects compound quickly: message wait times rise, broker memory usage climbs, and eventually producers are blocked or messages expire.

### Triage
1. Destinations dashboard: identify the affected destination(s) and trend.
2. Check enqueue vs dequeue rates (`activemq.message.enqueued` rate / `activemq.message.dequeued` rate). A persistent gap confirms consumer lag.
3. Check `activemq.consumer.count` on affected queues — zero or too few consumers is a common root cause.
4. Inspect `activemq.queue.message.inflight` for slow/stuck consumers holding unacked messages.

### Remediation
- Add consumers or fix slow consumer code paths.
- Shed load on the producer side if consumer capacity cannot grow.
- For bursty workloads, consider topics with selectors or sharding.

### Tuning
- `> 1000` is a conservative default; tune to your expected steady state (deep queues are acceptable for batch-style workloads).
- Lengthen the evaluation window to smooth bursty traffic.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-activemq.otel-*
// activemq.message.current is gauge; exclude advisory/system destinations
| WHERE activemq.message.current IS NOT NULL
  AND activemq.destination.name IS NOT NULL
  AND activemq.destination.name NOT IN ("ActiveMQ.DLQ", "ActiveMQ.Advisory.MasterBroker", "ActiveMQ.Advisory.Queue", "ActiveMQ.Advisory.Topic")
| STATS queue_depth = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(activemq.message.current)) BY activemq.broker.name, activemq.destination.name
// Alert when queue depth exceeds 1000; adjust for your expected throughput
| WHERE queue_depth > 1000
| SORT queue_depth DESC
| LIMIT 10
